prompt: In tool use (function calling), what actually happens when the model 'uses' a tool?
choices: The model emits a structured request, and the application executes the real function and returns the result
The model runs the function itself inside its weights
The tool replaces the model entirely for that turn
The application asks the model to write code that the user runs manually

prompt: Which steps make up one iteration of the agent loop? Select all that apply.
choices: Permanently retrain the model on the result

prompt: A PM is deciding between a single model call and a full agent for a new feature. Which situation most justifies using an agent?
choices: The task is multi-step and depends on fresh external data gathered along the way
The task is a one-shot summary of a fixed block of text
The team wants to reduce per-request cost as much as possible
The output must be a single deterministic label every time

prompt: Which of these are real risks of giving an agent autonomy, and which guardrails address them? Select all correct statements.
choices: Runaway loops can be limited with a maximum-iteration cap
Cost grows with each loop iteration, so timeouts and budgets help
An early wrong observation can compound through later steps
Adding more tools always eliminates the risk of runaway loops
